item.title: Revised Outsourcing Rulebook Module – Cloud Services
item.description: This revision to the CBUAE Outsourcing Module introduces mandatory cloud-specific provisions — including data residency requirements, right-to-audit clauses, and concentration risk controls — for licensed financial institutions using public cloud infrastructure. Institutions must conduct a gap assessment against the updated module and complete remediation within six months of publication.

obligations.description: Cloud outsourcing agreements must incorporate mandatory data residency clauses specifying that data is stored and processed within the UAE.
obligations.gap: The policy contains no provision on geographic data residency or UAE jurisdiction requirements for cloud-stored data.

obligations.description: Licensed institutions must contractually secure right-to-audit over all material cloud service providers.
obligations.gap: Policy mentions third-party oversight in general terms but does not mandate contractual audit rights for cloud providers specifically.

obligations.description: Licensed institutions must assess and document concentration risk arising from cloud service provider dependencies.
obligations.gap: Cloud concentration risk is not addressed; the policy’s operational risk section does not extend to cloud provider dependency.

obligations.description: All material cloud outsourcing arrangements must receive board-level approval prior to go-live.
obligations.gap: The policy requires board approval for material outsourcing generally but does not treat cloud arrangements as a distinct governance category.

obligations.description: Licensed institutions must complete a gap assessment against the updated Outsourcing Module cloud provisions and remediate all gaps within six months of publication.
obligations.gap: The policy has no mechanism for tracking regulatory module revisions or mandating gap assessments upon publication.

obligations.description: Licensed FIs must maintain a register of material outsourcing arrangements, including cloud service engagements.
obligations.evidence: Section 5: Outsourcing arrangements must be documented and recorded.
obligations.gap: The register requirement exists but does not extend to cloud services or require the additional disclosure fields mandated by the updated module.

obligations.description: Licensed FIs must conduct a risk assessment prior to entering into cloud outsourcing arrangements and perform ongoing monitoring of those arrangements.
obligations.evidence: Section 4: Risk assessments are required before entering into material outsourcing arrangements.
obligations.gap: The existing requirement covers outsourcing generally; it does not address cloud-specific risk factors (e.g., multi-tenancy, portability) or the enhanced ongoing monitoring cadence in the updated module.

obligations.description: Licensed institutions must ensure accountability for all outsourced activities, including cloud services, remains within the institution and cannot be delegated to the service provider.
obligations.evidence: Section 3: The institution remains responsible and accountable for all outsourced activities; accountability may not be delegated.
